summary.intent: Bring the Moltis Microsoft Teams channel implementation up to parity with the openclaw reference implementation, then improve onboarding, documentation, and web UI for Teams setup.
summary.outcome: Implemented 20+ missing features (JWT auth, retry/backoff, Adaptive Cards, edit-in-place streaming, Graph API tools, attachment handling, per-channel config overrides), added full documentation, integrated Tailscale/ngrok public URL detection into the Teams setup flow, and kept the branch current through three merges from main.

repo: Credentials use secrecy::Secret<String> throughout; moltis_msteams is notably stricter than openclaw which uses plain strings in some places
HTTP clients are created via moltis_common::http_client::build_default_http_client(), not reqwest::Client::new() directly — main added this convention mid-session causing a merge conflict
Channel plugins implement trait boundaries in crates/channels/src/plugin.rs; descriptor flags like supports_threads/supports_reactions/supports_interactive control gateway behavior
Config validation in validate.rs uses Map(Box::new(Leaf)) for channel account configs — semantic validation happens at serde deserialization time, not in the validator
The gateway tool registry is populated in crates/gateway/src/server.rs; tools hold Arc<RwLock<Plugin>> references for account resolution
JS formatting is enforced by biome; Rust formatting by rustfmt via cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt — both run as pre-push CI checks

The onboarding has a step ordering: Import → LLM → Voice → Channel → Identity → Summary; Remote Access (Tailscale/ngrok) was added to main before Channel, fixing the Teams public URL chicken-and-egg problem
preferredPublicBaseUrl() is a shared JS helper added by main that aggregates tunnel status across Tailscale and ngrok

code.finding: RedactedConfig had hardcoded field count of 9 but actually had 14 always-present fields — would cause runtime serialization panics when API returns channel config
std::sync::RwLockWriteGuard held across .await points in welcomed_conversations handling — makes the future non-Send, breaking tokio::spawn. Must scope the guard to drop before any await
Teams webhook handler must return 202 Accepted immediately and process in tokio::spawn to avoid Teams retry storms on slow LLM responses (~15s timeout before Teams retries)
rfind(' ') in chunk splitter returns the space position (e.g. 5 for 'word1 word2'), not the end of the word — test expectations must account for this off-by-one

The channels page (not onboarding) has no Remote Access step, so Tailscale Funnel detection logic must be kept inline in the AddTeamsModal; the simplification applied to onboarding-view.js does not apply here
Default offered channels list required updating to include 'msteams'; test assertions checked the exact list and needed updating in parallel

workflow: Parallel subagent exploration (one per codebase) is effective for comparison tasks — avoids context window pollution from large codebases
Running cargo check on the specific crate first (not the full workspace) gives faster feedback during iterative fixes
biome check --write must be run on JS files before committing; a missed run caused a CI failure that was caught only after push
Clippy's unwrap_used lint fires on test code — suppress with #[allow(clippy::unwrap_used)] on test functions rather than changing test logic
When resolving merge conflicts in JS files, conflicts are often additive (both sides adding new signals/state) and can be kept together rather than choosing one side
pub(crate) methods that are API surface but not yet called from outside generate dead_code warnings — suppress with #[allow(dead_code)] rather than removing the methods

friction: Three separate merge-from-main operations were needed during the session; the third had 5 conflicts across 5 files requiring careful manual resolution
tokio::spawn Send bounds caught late — the RwLockWriteGuard-across-await issue only surfaced when compiling the httpd crate, not the msteams crate
Biome formatting failure in CI after a push where biome was not run on page-channels.js post-merge — required a follow-up fix commit
RedactedConfig hardcoded field count was a silent bug that would only manifest at runtime — no compile-time or test coverage caught it
The Tailscale-in-TeamsForm vs preferredPublicBaseUrl() from main caused duplicated logic after the second merge that needed cleanup
Multiple rounds of clippy fixes required a subagent dispatch because the issue count was large enough to risk missing items
open_items: ngrok native Rust SDK integration (ngrok crate) as a public access option alongside Tailscale Funnel — intentionally deferred to a separate PR
Localization (fr/zh) for Teams-specific UI strings — all channels share this gap, not Teams-specific
E2E Playwright tests for Teams channel setup flow — no channel currently has these
Wiring teams_search_messages, teams_pin_message, teams_member_info tools to actual agent invocation paths — tools are registered but not yet surfaced in agent prompts
Graph API permissions documentation (Chat.Read.All, User.Read.All, Chat.ReadWrite.All) not yet reflected in the CLI bootstrap output or web UI setup checklist
